answer:
**Claude.ai Chat (Projects & Artifacts)**
*   **What it is:** The web-based conversational interface for Claude [1, 2]. It includes **Projects**, which are persistent, retrieval-augmented workspaces where you can upload reference files and set custom instructions [3, 4], and **Artifacts**, a feature that generates standalone, interactive digital assets like single-page HTML sites, React components, SVGs, and Mermaid diagrams in a dedicated side panel [5-7]. 
*   **Who it's for:** General users, writers, researchers, marketers, and teams who need a "thinking partner" to keep a human in the loop on every turn [8-10].
*   **Tasks it's best at:** Long-form writing, deep document analysis, interactive UI prototyping, brainstorming, and research synthesis [10-12].
*   **2026 Numbers:** Available on the Free tier, Pro ($20/month), Max 5x ($100/month), Max 20x ($200/month), and Team/Enterprise plans [13, 14]. Projects support up to **20 files (30MB each)** [3]. Context windows sit at **200K tokens** for Pro/Team and **500K tokens** for Enterprise [3]. 
*   **Concrete Tactics:** Build a "voice of the customer" Project by uploading survey data to maintain brand consistency across sessions [15]. For Artifacts, if you want to explore different design directions, edit your prior chat messages to branch the conversation—Claude will create a new version of the Artifact without losing your previous work [16].

**Claude Code**
*   **What it is:** An agentic software engineering tool that runs directly in your terminal and integrated development environments (VS Code, JetBrains) [17-19]. It maps your project, autonomously edits files, executes shell commands, runs tests, and stages Git commits [19-22].
*   **Who it's for:** Software engineers, quantitative developers, and technical product managers [8, 23, 24].
*   **Tasks it's best at:** Multi-file refactoring, tracing dependencies, debugging CI/CD pipeline failures, and executing complex feature builds [22, 25, 26].
*   **2026 Numbers:** Scored an industry-high **64.3% on SWE-bench Pro** and **87.6% on SWE-bench Verified** [27, 28]. As of May 2026, Claude authors **over 80% of Anthropic's own production code**, driving an 8-fold increase in daily lines merged per engineer [29]. 
*   **Concrete Tactics:** Place a `CLAUDE.md` markdown file in your project root to give the agent standing rules and architectural guidelines [30, 31]. Mid-session, use `Shift+Tab` to switch into **Plan Mode**, allowing Claude to explore the codebase and propose a detailed architectural plan without modifying your source files [32].

**Claude Cowork**
*   **What it is:** A desktop-based autonomous agent for knowledge workers [8, 33]. It uses the same agentic architecture as Claude Code but features a non-technical graphical interface. Cowork operates in an isolated local virtual machine (VM), allowing it to read, create, and edit files across your local folders without requiring a terminal [34, 35].
*   **Who it's for:** Non-technical professionals, operations teams, financial analysts, and marketers [8, 35, 36].
*   **Tasks it's best at:** Automating messy digital labor, organizing downloaded files, generating formatted Excel workbooks with real formulas, cross-referencing PDFs into PowerPoint decks, and running scheduled reports [37-39].
*   **2026 Numbers:** Generally available as of April 2026 for paid plans [40, 41]. Early adopters (e.g., Brainlabs, PwC) reported reducing 6-week analysis tasks to 2.5 days [42] and achieving 40% productivity boosts [43].
*   **Concrete Tactics:** Type `/schedule` in a Cowork task to automate recurring work [44]—such as extracting analytics every Monday morning [39]. Provide Claude with a directory of raw receipt images and instruct it to build a formatted expense report spreadsheet; it will autonomously process the batch [45].

**Claude Desktop App**
*   **What it is:** The native application for macOS (v11+) and Windows (x64) that unifies the Chat, Code, and Cowork tabs into one interface [17, 46, 47]. It integrates deeply with your local system to bypass browser sandboxing [48].
*   **Who it's for:** Power users and daily professionals who want to eliminate the friction of switching browser tabs [49].
*   **Tasks it's best at:** Real-time support during document creation, fast un-siloed AI access, and local Model Context Protocol (MCP) integrations [50].
*   **2026 Numbers:** The app uses just **~180–250 MB of memory**, compared to 1.2–2.0+ GB for browser tabs, and launches in **~3 seconds** [51].
*   **Concrete Tactics:** Use the **Quick Entry** feature (default: double-tap the Option key on macOS) to pull up Claude over any active application, allowing you to highlight text in an email and ask Claude to rewrite it without switching apps [52, 53]. Install local MCP extensions with a single click to grant Claude direct access to your Slack or Google Drive [53, 54].

**Claude Mobile App (with Dispatch)**
*   **What it is:** The iOS and Android mobile app, recently upgraded with the **Dispatch** and **Remote Control** frameworks [55-57]. It bridges your phone and your desktop, allowing you to trigger local desktop actions from anywhere [55, 58]. 
*   **Who it's for:** Busy executives, founders, and developers away from their keyboards who need to orchestrate or monitor tasks on the go [56, 59].
*   **Tasks it's best at:** Dictating tasks during a commute, monitoring parallel Claude Code sessions, reviewing diffs, and approving agent permissions remotely [59-61].
*   **2026 Numbers:** Features a unified syncing thread that routes to local environments in real-time [55]. When using Dispatch to trigger computer use tasks, app approvals expire after **30 minutes** for security [62]. 
*   **Concrete Tactics:** Use **Dispatch** from your phone to dictate a task like "Find the pannacotta PDF on my desktop and Slack it to the team"; Claude will wake up your desktop, run the local search in Cowork, and send you a push notification when finished [59, 63, 64]. If you want to pull a mobile conversation into your local terminal, run `claude --teleport` [61, 65].

**Claude API**
*   **What it is:** Programmatic access to Anthropic's 2026 model family (Opus 4.8, Fable 5, Sonnet 4.6, Haiku 4.5) via Anthropic directly, Amazon Bedrock, Google Vertex AI, and Microsoft Foundry [66-68].
*   **Who it's for:** Enterprise architects, system integrators, backend developers, and AI application builders [56, 68, 69].
*   **Tasks it's best at:** Powering custom AI features, running high-volume asynchronous background jobs, deploying enterprise compliance boundaries (Zero Data Retention), and executing programmatic tool calling [56, 68, 70].
*   **2026 Numbers:** 
    *   **Opus 4.8**: $5.00 input / $25.00 output per million tokens (MTok) [23, 66]. Fast Mode available for 2.5x speed at $10/$50 [66].
    *   **Fable 5**: $10.00 / $50.00 per MTok (for multi-day autonomous projects) [23, 71].
    *   **Sonnet 4.6**: $3.00 / $15.00 per MTok [23, 66].
    *   **Haiku 4.5**: $1.00 / $5.00 per MTok [23, 66].
    *   The **1-million-token context window** is standard for 4.6+ generation models at no premium surcharge [20, 66].
*   **Concrete Tactics:** Combine the **Batch API** (which offers a flat 50% discount for asynchronous jobs completing within 24 hours) with **Prompt Caching** (which cuts cache read costs to 0.1x of the base price). This combination can reduce total input token costs for large-scale operations by up to **95%** [21, 72, 73]. Additionally, deploy an MCP gateway to standardize your external database and tool connections across all API endpoints rather than hardcoding integrations [74, 75].

Projects serve as isolated, retrieval-augmented workspaces designed to eliminate the repetitive task of copying and pasting context across multiple sessions.[9, 10] Within a project, users can upload up to 20 reference files, with each file capped at a maximum size of 30 MB.[5, 9, 10] These may include style guides, system specifications, codebase snapshots, or raw datasets.[9, 10] The system also allows the ingestion of persistent instructions that Claude must respect throughout every interaction inside that workspace.[9, 10]
Key specs (API):

Artifacts is Claude's output format for code, documents, diagrams, and interactive content that can be rendered, edited, and exported directly from the conversation interface. When Claude generates substantial standalone content – code, HTML, SVG, Mermaid diagrams, React components, or formatted Markdown – a side panel opens with a live preview. Users can iterate on artifacts, share them publicly, or (on Team and Enterprise) share within organizational boundaries. Artifacts launched in preview June 2024 and reached general availability across all tiers on August 26, 2024. As of April 2026, Artifacts ships on all paid plans and inside Projects.

Artifacts are self-contained digital assets generated by Claude that render in a dedicated, interactive side panel adjacent to the main conversation window.[9, 11] The system automatically triggers an artifact when the model determines that the requested output is substantial (typically exceeding 15 lines of content), represents a standalone utility, or is intended for external iteration and reuse.[12]
This guide walks through each product, offers customer examples from across the industry,  and tips on how to roll out Claude inside your firm.

This guide gives you the short answer first, then the reasoning, then a framework for deciding which surface fits a given piece of work. It closes on the part most comparisons skip: once any of these tools can act on its own, the question stops being which tool and becomes how you govern what it does.

The current anthropic.com/pricing page lists “Includes Claude Code” under Pro; an independent changelog tracker (scriptbyai.com, April 2026) states Anthropic removed Claude Code from Pro in April 2026. Conflict unresolved. Max plans confirmed to include Claude Code; Enterprise confirmed; API access via Claude Code SDK is uniformly available.

(Pricing as of June 2026 — verify before purchasing. Annual billing: Claude Pro ~15% discount available. Google AI Pro 50% promo for new subscribers. Businesses now spend an average $100–$5,000/month on AI tools per Zylo's 2026 AI Cost Analysis.)

Within Anthropic’s own engineering organization, Claude authors and merges more than 80% of all production code.[10] Prior to the launch of Claude Code, this figure was in the low single digits.[10] This transition has driven an 8-fold increase in daily lines of code merged per engineer compared to the 2021–2024 baseline, primarily because human developers now focus on reviewing and steering the agent rather than writing code manually.[10] In organization-wide polls, engineers self-report using Claude in 59% of their daily work, achieving an average +50% productivity boost.[11] Power users, representing 14% of the engineering staff, report productivity gains exceeding 100%.[11]

Getting started is light for a developer: Node.js and a Claude account are the core prerequisites. The features that matter at scale are the ones that keep autonomy in check. Plan Mode lets Claude propose an approach before it touches anything. Checkpoints let you roll back. Sub-agents and agent teams let you split a large task across focused workers. A CLAUDE.md file in your project root gives it the standing context and rules it should follow every session.
In CI, you can automate code review and issue triage with GitHub Actions or GitLab CI/CD .

Multi-Agent Orchestration: For complex refactoring, developers can instruct Claude to spawn sub-agents to investigate separate parts of the codebase.[7] These sub-agents run in isolated context windows, returning a clean summary of their findings to the main agent to prevent context bloating.[7] Developers can also build custom agents with granular tool permissions using the Python or TypeScript Agent SDK.[7]

The program was developed by Boris Cherny over a ten-day period using Claude Code, demonstrating the recursive capabilities of agentic frameworks.[1, 5] While Claude Code remains optimized for software developers running terminal-based commands, Claude Cowork is designed for non-technical business professionals who require multi-step file manipulation, application orchestration, and document compilation without terminal interaction.[2, 4, 6]
Use Claude Code when

The workflow is simple to describe. You point Cowork at a folder, you describe an outcome, and it plans and executes the steps to get there, returning a finished deliverable rather than instructions. Fill an expense report from a folder of receipt images. Turn a stack of notes into a first-draft report. Reorganize a messy directory. Build a spreadsheet and a dashboard from raw data. It reads, edits, and creates files in the folders you grant it, and it can extend its reach through plugins and connectors over the same Model Context Protocol that Claude Code uses.
Reach for Claude Code when the output is software

In most pilots, Claude's product surfaces come online in a specific order. Skills and plugins come first because they're low-risk and high-reuse. The Microsoft 365 add-ins come next, extending what a pilot team has built into Excel, PowerPoint, Word, and Outlook,where the bulk of front- and back-office work actually happens. Claude Cowork tends to come in at the back end of the pilot, when the team's ready to move from single-document work to project-level work that spans files and apps.
